```python
class Solution:
    def countDigitOne(self, n: int) -> int:
        res = 0
        i = 1
        while i <= n:
            divider = i * 10
            res += (n // divider) * i + min(max(n % divider - i + 1, 0), i)
            i *= 10
        return res
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates through each digit position, calculating the number of times the digit 1 appears at that position in all numbers up to n. It uses mathematical formulas to efficiently calculate these counts, resulting in a time complexity of O(log n) due to the number of iterations being proportional to the number of digits in n.
